differentials.name: Extensive crush injuries with associated muscle damage and ensuing renal insufficiency
differentials.raw: Extensive crush injuries with associated muscle damage and ensuing renal insufficiency (from motionless recumbency)

differentials.name: 12 mmHg increase in pulmonary capillary wedge pressure
differentials.raw: 12 mmHg increase in pulmonary capillary wedge pressure (= direct toxic effect on myocardium)

context: Cause:    organized thrombi in small veins causes an increase in peripheral resistance and hydrostatic pressure √  enlarged pulmonary arteries √  diffuse interstitial edema + numerous Kerley lines √  peribronchial cuffing √  dilated right ventricle Permeability Edema Heroin-induced Pulmonary Edema Hx:  overdose of opiates (almost exclusively with heroin, rarely with cocaine / "crack") Frequency:   15% of cases of heroin overdose Pathophysiology:   depression of medullary respiratory center leading to hypoxia + acidosis √  widespread patchy bilateral airspace consolidations √  ill-defined vesse
